candidates.name: Jake Lang
candidates.bio: Edward Jacob Lang (born 1995–96) is an American far-right activist and politician. He was a participant in the 2021 January 6 United States Capitol attack, for which he served four years in prison. Lang's charges were dismissed by President Donald Trump in his pardon of attack defendants on the first day of his second presidency in 2025.

candidates.name: Josue Economist Larose
candidates.bio: The 2026 United States Senate special election in Florida is expected to be held on November 3, 2026, to elect a member of the United States Senate to represent the state of Florida, to finish the last two years of former Republican Senator Marco Rubio's term, who resigned his seat to become United States secretary of state in President Donald Trump's second administration. The primary election will be held August 18, 2026.

candidates.name: Ashley Moody
candidates.bio: Ashley Brooke Moody (born March 28, 1975) is an American politician and attorney serving since 2025 as the junior United States senator from Florida. A member of the Republican Party, she served from 2019 to 2025 as the 38th attorney general of Florida.

top_stances.summary: Oppose congressional disapproval of military actions; support executive discretion in Iran operations.
top_stances.track_record_note: voted NAY on sjres184-119 and sjres114-119 opposing removal of armed forces from Iran hostilities, but YEA on sjres124-119 supporting removal from Cuba hostilities

top_stances.summary: Oppose congressional disapproval of employment authorization rule changes.
top_stances.track_record_note: voted NAY on sjres99-119 blocking disapproval of USCIS employment authorization removal rule

top_stances.summary: Support pro-gun law enforcement leadership and ATF director nominations.
top_stances.track_record_note: voted YEA on confirmation and cloture for Robert Cekada as ATF Director

candidates.name: Ernest Ernie John Rev. Dr. Rivera
Marco Rubio
candidates.bio: Marco Antonio Rubio (/ˈruː.bi.oʊ/, ROO-bee-oh; born May 28, 1971) is an American politician, attorney, and diplomat serving since 2025 as the 72nd United States secretary of state. He is also the acting national security advisor. A member of the Republican Party, Rubio represented Florida in the United States Senate from 2011 to 2025.
key_messages: Rubio supports balancing the federal budget while prioritizing defense spending, and proposes setting corporate taxes at 25% with a reformed tax code and capped economic regulations.
Rubio opposes the Affordable Care Act and has voted to repeal it.

Rubio initially supported comprehensive immigration reform with a pathway to citizenship for undocumented immigrants, but later developed more hardline views on immigration and rejected bipartisan reform efforts.

Rubio initially rejected the scientific consensus on climate change but reversed this position by 2018, acknowledging that humans contribute to increased greenhouse gases and rising sea levels, and joined the bipartisan Climate Solutions Caucus in 2020.
Rubio supports expanding public charter schools, opposes Common Core State Standards, and advocates closing the federal Department of Education.
Rubio has said that gun control laws consistently fail to achieve their purpose and has defended accepting contributions from the National Rifle Association.

Rubio has expressed caution about criminal justice reform focused on drug crimes and would be open to legalizing non-psychoactive cannabis for medical use but otherwise opposes legalization and would enforce federal law against state-legalized cannabis.

Rubio's foreign policy approach is interventionist and hawkish, supporting military interventions, tough sanctions on Iran, opposition to Syrian refugees post-2015, support for Taiwan and Hong Kong democracy, and strong opposition to China and the Chinese Communist Party.
Rubio supports setting corporate taxes at 25% and reforming the tax code, while also proposing to increase the social security retirement age based on longer life expectancy.

campaign_themes.heading: economy
campaign_themes.text: Rubio supports balancing the federal budget while prioritizing defense spending, and proposes setting corporate taxes at 25% with a reformed tax code and capped economic regulations.

campaign_themes.heading: healthcare
campaign_themes.text: Rubio opposes the Affordable Care Act and has voted to repeal it.

campaign_themes.text: Rubio initially supported comprehensive immigration reform with a pathway to citizenship for undocumented immigrants, but later developed more hardline views on immigration and rejected bipartisan reform efforts.

campaign_themes.heading: climate
campaign_themes.text: Rubio initially rejected the scientific consensus on climate change but reversed this position by 2018, acknowledging that humans contribute to increased greenhouse gases and rising sea levels, and joined the bipartisan Climate Solutions Caucus in 2020.

campaign_themes.heading: education
campaign_themes.text: Rubio supports expanding public charter schools, opposes Common Core State Standards, and advocates closing the federal Department of Education.

campaign_themes.heading: guns
campaign_themes.text: Rubio has said that gun control laws consistently fail to achieve their purpose and has defended accepting contributions from the National Rifle Association.

campaign_themes.heading: criminal_justice
campaign_themes.text: Rubio has expressed caution about criminal justice reform focused on drug crimes and would be open to legalizing non-psychoactive cannabis for medical use but otherwise opposes legalization and would enforce federal law against state-legalized cannabis.

campaign_themes.heading: foreign_policy
campaign_themes.text: Rubio's foreign policy approach is interventionist and hawkish, supporting military interventions, tough sanctions on Iran, opposition to Syrian refugees post-2015, support for Taiwan and Hong Kong democracy, and strong opposition to China and the Chinese Communist Party.

campaign_themes.heading: taxes
campaign_themes.text: Rubio supports setting corporate taxes at 25% and reforming the tax code, while also proposing to increase the social security retirement age based on longer life expectancy.

platform_excerpts.excerpt: Rubio supports balancing the federal budget, while prioritizing defense spending.
Rubio opposes the Affordable Care Act (Obamacare) and has voted to repeal it.

Over time, Rubio distanced himself from his previous efforts to reach a compromise on immigration, and developed more hardline views on immigration, rejecting bipartisan immigration reform efforts in 2018.

He reversed this position by 2018, saying that humans contribute to an increase in greenhouse gases and sea levels are rising at a measurable rate. In 2020, he joined the bipartisan Climate Solutions Caucus.
He supports expanding public charter schools, opposes Common Core State Standards, and advocates closing the federal Department of Education.
He has said that gun control laws consistently fail to achieve their purpose.
He has said that he would be open to legalizing non-psychoactive forms of cannabis for medical use, but otherwise opposes its legalization for recreational and medical purposes.
Rubio's foreign policy approach has been described as 'interventionist' and 'hawkish'. He supported the 2003 invasion of Iraq and military intervention in Libya.
Rubio supports setting corporate taxes at 25%, reforming the tax code, and capping economic regulations, and proposes to increase the social security retirement age based on longer life expectancy.

top_stances.summary: Interventionist approach: military action, Iran sanctions, Taiwan support, strong China opposition.
Hardline stance rejecting bipartisan reform; moved from earlier pathway-to-citizenship position.
Corporate tax rate at 25%, reformed tax code, raise Social Security retirement age.
Expand charter schools, oppose Common Core, close federal Department of Education.
Oppose Affordable Care Act; has voted for repeal.
Gun control laws ineffective; defend NRA contributions; oppose new restrictions.
Humans contribute to climate change; acknowledge greenhouse gases and sea level rise.
Balance federal budget prioritizing defense; cap economic regulations; reform tax code.
Cautious on drug crime reform; open to medical cannabis only; enforce federal law.

candidates.name: Rick Sen Scott
candidates.bio: Richard Lynn  Scott (né Myers; born December 1, 1952) is an American attorney, businessman, politician, and Navy veteran serving as the senior United States senator from Florida, a seat he has held since 2019.[a] A member of the Republican Party, he served from 2011 to 2019 as the 45th governor of Florida.

top_stances.summary: Support confirmation of ATF director nominee.

candidates.name: Alix Christopher Mr. Jr. Toulme
candidates.bio: The 2026 United States Senate special election in Florida is expected to be held on November 3, 2026, to elect a member of the United States Senate to represent the state of Florida, to finish the last two years of former Republican Senator Marco Rubio's term, who resigned his seat to become United States secretary of state in President Donald Trump's second administration. The primary election will be held August 18, 2026.
